title: Building integrated photovoltaic system with glass photovoltaic tiles
abstract: Building integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) systems provide for solar panel arrays with improved aesthetics and efficiency that can replace a conventional roof surface structure. These BIPV systems can utilize photovoltaic PV roof tiles defined as glass tiles having photovoltaic elements embedded or incorporated into the body of the roof tile. Such PV roof tiles can include one or more lapping features for interfacing with adjacent tiles and features for electrically connecting multiples tiles within a course to an external power optimizer. Such PV roof tiles can utilize stamped glass that is stamped to define these features within an integrated glass tile and can further include texture, striations on the glass tile and/or color matched back layers or various other components to obscure visibility of any embedded solar cells and provide a more pleasing appearance.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A PV roof tile comprising: one or more solar cells; a planar glass member overlaying the one or more solar cells; a substrate supporting the one or more solar cells within a cavity and affixed to the planar glass member so as to encase the one or more solar cells therebetween; and a plurality of electrical contacts electrically coupled with the one or more solar cells that are accessible from an exterior of the planar glass member and substrate affixed thereto, wherein the plurality of electrical contacts are disposed within a connector disposed along an up-roof portion of the PV roof tile that is configured to be accessible for connection with a corresponding connector from an up-roof direction when the PV roof tile is mounted on or within a roof. 2. The PV roof tile of claim 1, wherein the roof tile includes a lapped region along one lateral side dimensioned to underlie an adjacent PV roof tile when installed within a course. 3. The PV roof tile of claim 1, wherein the planar glass member has a striated or textured surface such that one or more solar cells are visually obscured, partially or fully when viewed from a non-normal (< or > 90 degrees) viewing angle. 4. The PV roof tile of claim 1, further comprising: a wire bussing electrically connected to the one or more solar cells of the PV roof tile via the connector, the wire bussing extending adjacent to and up-roof from the connector within the roof. 5. The PV roof tile of claim 1 , wherein the solar panel comprises: the up-roof portion being defined such that an up-roof course of like solar roof tiles or ridge cap tiles overlay the up-roof portion when mounted in a solar roof tile assembly; and a down-roof portion in which the one or more solar cells are disposed and that remains exposed when mounted in the solar roof tile assembly. 6. The PV roof tile of claim 1 , wherein the connector comprises a positive and negative lead contact such that opposing lateral sides and an underside of the PV roof tile are without any electrical connectors for the one or more solar cells of the PV roof tile. 7. The PV roof tile of claim 6, wherein the connector is disposed along an up-roof edge of the roof tile. 8. The PV roof tile of claim 1, wherein connector is disposed between a pair of mechanical fastening features disposed within the up-roof portion, the pair of mechanical fastening features configured to facilitate mounting of the PV roof tile on or within the roof. 9. The PV roof tile of claim 8, wherein the plurality of electrical contacts comprise any of: exposed wires, metal tabs, a female connector, a male connector. 10. The PV roof tile of claim 1, wherein the plurality of electrical contacts are disposed in a connector on an on underside of the solar roof tile, each being configured to electrically connect with a busbar within a mounting track configured for mounting on a roof. 11. A PV roof tile comprising: one or more solar cells; a planar glass member overlaying the one or more solar cells; wherein the one or more solar cells are encapsulated against the planar glass member by one or more under layers; and a plurality of electrical contacts electrically coupled with the one or more solar cells that are accessible from an exterior of the planar glass member and substrate affixed thereto, wherein the plurality of electrical contacts are disposed within a connector along an up-roof portion of the PV roof tile that is configured to be accessible for connection with a corresponding connector from an up-roof direction when the PV roof tile is mounted on or within a roof. 12. The PV roof tile of claim 11, wherein the one or more layers comprise a first layer adjacent the one or more solar cells and a second layer underlying the first layer, wherein the first and second layers are different materials. 13. The PV roof tile of claim 12, wherein the first layer is formed of EVA and the second layer is a protective layer. 14. The PV roof tile of claim 11 , further comprising: 

 a wire bussing electrically connected to the one or more solar cells and extending to the plurality of electrical contacts. 15. The PV roof tile of claim 11, wherein the solar panel comprises: the up-roof portion being defined such that the up-roof portion is overlaid by an up-roof course of like solar roof tiles or ridge cap tiles when mounted in a solar roof tile assembly; and a down-roof portion in which the one or more solar cells are disposed and that remains exposed when mounted in the solar roof tile assembly. 16. The PV roof tile of claim 11, wherein the connector is disposed on an up-roof edge of the PV roof tile. 17. A building integrated photovoltaic system comprising: a first plurality of PV roof tiles, each being according to claim 1, that are electrically connected in a string when arranged within a first course on a roof surface; a first wire bussing electrically connected to each connector of the plurality of PV roof tiles; and a first optimizer coupled with the wire bussing. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the first wire bussing between the first plurality of PV roof tiles is configured for carrying low voltage, the system further including: a high voltage wiring electrically connected to the optimizer for carrying a high voltage DC or a high voltage AC to a main panel of a building on which the system is installed. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein the first wire bussing and the plurality of electrical contacts of the first plurality of PV roof tiles are configured such that at least some of the first plurality of PV roof tiles are electrically connected within the string when arranged within a second course down-roof of the first course. 20. The system of claim 17, further comprising: a second plurality of PV roof tiles, each being according to claim 1, that are electrically connected in a string when arranged within a second course down-roof and adjacent the first course on the roof surface; a second wire bussing electrically connected to each connector of the plurality of PV roof tiles. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the first and second bussing are both electrically connected to the first optimizer. 22. The system of claim 20, wherein the second bussing is electrically connected to a second optimizer. 23. The PV roof tile of claim 1, further comprising a wiring harness having a number of taps and terminating in a DC optimizer, the number of taps limiting the aggregate voltage of all connected PV roof tiles to an amount less than 50 volts. 24. The PV roof tile of claim 11 , further comprising a wiring harness having a number of taps and terminating in a DC optimizer, the number of taps limiting the aggregate voltage of all connected PV roof tiles to an amount less than 50 volts. 25. The system of claim 17, the first wiring bussing comprising a wiring harness having a number of taps for connecting to a plurality of PV roof tiles and terminating in the first optimizer. 26. The system of claim 17, the first wiring bussing comprising a busbar track.
